rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 20, 2026, line 12. REAL PARTY IN INTEREST ENA REALES' Motion To Quash Service Of Writ Of Mandate. Off calendar as untimely filed and served. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 20, 2026, line 12. PETETION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. Petitioner Sarah Lewis seeks a writ of mandate to compel the Small Claims Division of the San Francisco Superior Court to rule on her request for reconsideration. The writ is denied without prejudice to Lewis's noticing her hearing in the proper department. Lewis has noticed the writ for hearing in Department 301 of this court, a law and motion department. A petition for writ of mandate relating to a small claims action other than one that relates to a postjudgment enforcement order "may be heard by a judge who is assigned to the appellate division of the superior court" (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 116.798, subd. (a)(1)), or "by the court of appeal or by the Supreme Court" (id., subd. (a)(2)). The law and motion department is not designated as an appellate division. Lewis must notice her writ for hearing in the appellate division of the superior court. The request for writ is denied without prejudice. Because the court is not reaching the merits of the writ petition, it does not pass on the argument raised in real party's motion to quash that service was not proper. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 20, 2026, line 5. DEFENDANT THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents. Defendant The Bay Clubs Company LLC's motion to compel further responses and production of documents from plaintiff Golden Gateway Commons I Residential Association is denied as moot. The HOA's argument, made at hearing on the ex parte motion, that the demurrer might moot this motion was apt. The Bay Clubs' demurrer on grounds of standing was sustained and the HOA is no longer in the case. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KARMIN JAL GUZDER VS. JOSHUA LEE ROOFENER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Stay Action Pending Contractual Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 20, 2026, line 10. PLAINTIFF KARMIN GUZDER AS TRUSTEE'S Motion To Stay Action Pending Contractual Arbitration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
